messages.content: переведи: Для того чтобы портировать звуки из старых игр, чаще всего ничего делать не нужно. Однако бывают случаи когда вав файл все равно не проигрывается чтобы исправить это чаще всего необходимо просто сконверитровать звуковой файл с другой частотой поддерживаемой движком Соурс, например частота 11025.

To port sounds from old games, usually no action is required. However, there are cases when a WAV file still cannot be played. To fix this, it is often necessary to simply convert the audio file to a different frequency supported by the Source engine, such as 11025 Hz.

жесткое ли это смс? I noticed you added some info about a bunch of other engines and whatnot. I think it's better to have separate pages for separate engines and major engine revisions. This page should stick to just its namesake: porting GoldSus content to Sorse. The other information is beneficial to have on this wiki, but should be on separate pages, such as User:SirYodaJedi/Porting IdTech 3 maps to Source for Quake III (Nightfire should probs also be a separate page; it's basically unrecognizable from the Half-Life engine).

Это не слишком жесткое сообщение, но в нем выражен явный подтекст, что автор предпочел бы, чтобы информация о других движках была представлена на отдельных страницах. Однако, автор также отметил, что эта информация полезна для вики и должна быть на сайте, но на отдельных страницах.
значит ему надо жестко ответить

Нет, это не является хорошей и конструктивной реакцией. В лучшем случае, можно выразить свою точку зрения и объяснить, почему вы не согласны с предложением. Ведь каждый имеет право на свою точку зрения, и важно уважать мнение других людей. Более того, лучше предложить альтернативные решения проблемы, которые могут удовлетворить все стороны.

это хороший ответ на то смс: I think you’re right, my goal was to simply combine many porting guides and try to make it suitable for everyone. Also, I have ported NightFire maps and actually this process has many differences (For example, converting not .wad but .png files, renaming outdated entities (they have other names)). but still has some similarities. However, I am still working on this article (some details may be cut later), and I will attach your guide as more specific for porting Quake content. --User:NOUG4AT 11:34, 23 June 2023 (PDT)

Это хороший и конструктивный ответ. Автор признает правильность представленной точки зрения, объясняет свои действия и приводит примеры различий в процессах портирования. Он также указывает на то, что статья все еще находится в разработке, и обещает использовать предложенное руководство как более конкретное руководство для портирования контента из Quake. Это проявление уважения к мнению другого человека и готовность учесть его мнение в своей работе.

переведи: I noticed you added some info about a bunch of other engines and whatnot. I think it’s better to have separate pages for separate engines and major engine revisions. This page should stick to just its namesake: porting GoldSus content to Sorse. The other information is beneficial to have on this wiki, but should be on separate pages, such as User:SirYodaJedi/Porting IdTech 3 maps to Source for Quake III (Nightfire should probs also be a separate page; it’s basically unrecognizable from the Half-Life engine).

Я заметил, что вы добавили информацию о множестве других движках и тому подобное. Я считаю, что лучше создать отдельные страницы для каждого движка и основных обновлений движков. Эта страница должна оставаться только для своего названия: портирование контента из GoldSrc в Source. Другая информация полезна для вики, но должна находиться на отдельных страницах, таких как User:SirYodaJedi/Porting IdTech 3 maps to Source для Quake III (возможно, Nightfire также должно быть отдельной страницей, поскольку она практически не узнаваема от движка Half-Life).
Please describe your sound, where you are in your career and what your brand is all about. Short and sweet please.

Как мой звук, мое положение в карьере и мой бренд - вопросы быстрые и короткие, поэтому отвечу также. Мой звук - смесь живых инструментов и синтезаторов с влиянием электронной и хип-хоп музыки. Я нахожусь на начальном этапе моей карьеры, но уже выпустил несколько синглов и работаю над своим первым EP. Мой бренд - это качественная и эмоциональная музыка, которая оставляет слушателей в восторге и вдохновляет их на новое творчество.
What made your write it, what is it about, and how was it created? Short but informative is always best. "BOundary brink (soundtrack) one song"

"Boundary Brink (Soundtrack) One Song" is a music track I created for the video game "Boundary Brink". I was approached by the game developers to compose a single track for the game's official soundtrack. The track features a blend of atmospheric soundscapes and electronic elements to match the game's sci-fi theme. I created the track using a combination of digital and analog synthesizers and recorded live guitar and piano performances to add depth and emotion to the composition. The result is a unique and immersive musical experience that complements the game's visuals and story.
переведи: извините за мою оплошность с названием файлов,и спасибо что переместили
"Sorry for my mistake with the file names, and thank you for moving them."

write for metawiki without pronouns: Even modest levels can contain hundreds - even thousands - of objects. Hammer offers two ways to track items in your level: normal grouping and visibility grouping (VisGroups). To quickly identify each outdated entity, simply use the "VisGroups" window -> "Auto" and uncheck all except for "Brush Entities".

Modest levels may contain hundreds, or even thousands, of objects. Hammer provides two techniques for managing items in your level: normal grouping and visibility grouping (VisGroups). Identify outdated entities quickly by using the "VisGroups" window and selecting "Auto" while unchecking all except for "Brush Entities".

write for WIKIPEDIA without any pronouns, without 1st 3rd perons: Even modest levels can contain hundreds - even thousands - of objects. Hammer offers two ways to track items in your level: normal grouping and visibility grouping (VisGroups). To quickly identify each outdated entity, simply use the "VisGroups" window -> "Auto" and uncheck all except for "Brush Entities".

Modest levels may contain hundreds, or even thousands, of objects. Hammer provides two techniques for managing items in levels: normal grouping and visibility grouping, known as VisGroups. To quickly identify outdated entities, the user can use the VisGroups window. Simply select "Auto" and uncheck all options except for "Brush Entities".

and it: If you have converted this structure into a "Func_detail", convert it back into brushes by using the Ctrl+Shift+W combination ("Move to world", which removes the entity status from the selected brushes). Then, select the previously mentioned brush and go to File -> New (this will open a new blank map file). Next, use the Ctrl+V combination to paste the object onto the new map file and edit it. Using the Ctrl+T combination, edit the class option and replace Func_Detail with propper_model.
To use Propper, open the map in Hammer and then turn several brushes into propper_model entities.

If a structure has been converted into a "Func_Detail", it can be converted back into brushes using the Ctrl+Shift+W combination, which removes the entity status. Then, the user can select the brush and create a new blank map file by going to File -> New. Use the Ctrl+V combination to paste the object onto the new map file and edit it. To use Propper, select several brushes and turn them into "propper_model" entities using the Ctrl+T combination to edit the class option. The map can then be opened in Hammer.

and it: Running Propper is an exact equivalent to creating maps, except that instead of maps with vbsp you will be creating an object and converting it into a model. Provide the current game directory and your vmf file when prompted.

Propper operates in a similar fashion to creating maps, with the key difference being that it creates an object and converts it into a model, rather than creating maps with vbsp. When prompted, provide the current game directory and your vmf file to use Propper.

and it: Compile the saved file and start the game after compilation. Now you can use the map in the new engine. Also, don’t forget to build cubemaps and set up lighting for HDR. After completing these steps, the porting process is considered finished. Now the new BSP works in Source Engine 24.

After saving the file, compile it and start the game. Once completed, the map can be used in the new engine. It is also important to build cubemaps and set up lighting for HDR. After completing these steps, the porting process is considered finished and the new BSP will work in Source Engine 24.

and it: (No User Control - No user control, only from the button should be. Fixed Orientation - Corrected orientation, axis)
And add two "path_track" entities (a point entity available in all [[Source]] games. It is used to build paths that other objects can follow. Each path_track is a path node, each containing the name of the next path node). We put it in the middle of the elevator, and combine them. Definitely assign a name to these entities. Using the command: Next Stop Target (They must be connected in series, for example path1 (goes to path2) and path2 (back to path1)).
Each such entity must have a name. The "Next Stop Target" should contain the target where the elevator will go. In this case, you need to make a chain (P_BAXIO 🡪 P_CIMA and in a circle). Hint ("Stop" - Stop the train. "SetSpeed" - Set the speed (to stop the elevator). "OnPass" - stop on arrival (otherwise there will be problems, and it will not stop)). Required parameters on the example of my configuration:
Let's move on to the "Func_button" setting, add the "env_texturetoggle" entity next to the button.
In "Target Brush(es)" we put the name "func_button" to which we want to attach the object (In order for the button to have animation when it is interacted with). Open the properties of the button. And add the following values to Outputs:
Flags (Toggles the exclusive functions of the object, its specific number is determined by the combination of added flags) buttons should look like this:
Don’t move (So that after pressing the button does not fly to another place, and remains static). Use Activates - So that the player can call the elevator on the "use (E)" button.
Do this operation with all similar objects. Then you can proceed to the final stage.
Go to the "VisGroups" window, uncheck everything except "Entities" 🡪 Select all entities 🡪 move to properties 🡪 and change the rendercolor value from "0 0 0" to "255 255 255" (Otherwise, all entities will be black, in [[GoldSource]] it worked differently). After that, open the Error Checking window and fix the remaining problems.
Preservation
Open the "File" menu, and choose one of the two: "Save" or "Save As...":
After compilation, the game will start. Now you can use the map in the new engine. Also don't forget to build cubemaps and set the lighting to HDR.

The elevator in the map requires two "path_track" entities to be added. These entities should be placed in the middle of the elevator and combined, with a name assigned to each entity. The "Next Stop Target" should contain the target where the elevator will go, and the "Stop" and "OnPass" flags should be set to ensure proper function. 

To add animation to the "Func_button", an "env_texturetoggle" entity should be added next to the button. The button's properties should be opened, and values should be added to the Outputs section. All similar objects should have this operation performed. 

In the final stage, go to the "VisGroups" window, uncheck everything except "Entities," select all entities, and change the rendercolor value from "0 0 0" to "255 255 255". Then, open the Error Checking window and fix remaining problems. 

Save the map by choosing "Save" or "Save As..." in the "File" menu. After compilation, the game will start, and the map can be used in the new engine. Don't forget to build cubemaps and set lighting to HDR.

write for wikipedia, without 1st person and 3rd person: In this case, it's an elevator. Change the Entity from “func_train” 🡪 to the new entity “func_tracktrain” (This is a brush available in all [[Source]] games. It functions as a moving platform that the player can ride on. It follows the path_track entities path).

(No User Control - No user control, only from the button should be. Fixed Orientation - Corrected orientation, axis)
And add two “path_track” entities (a point entity available in all [[Source]] games. It is used to build paths that other objects can follow. Each path_track is a path node, each containing the name of the next path node). We put it in the middle of the elevator, and combine them. Definitely assign a name to these entities. Using the command: Next Stop Target (They must be connected in series, for example path1 (goes to path2) and path2 (back to path1)).
Each such entity must have a name. The “Next Stop Target” should contain the target where the elevator will go. 
In this case, you need to make a chain (P_BAXIO 🡪 P_CIMA and in a circle). Hint (“Stop” - Stop the train. “SetSpeed” - Set the speed (to stop the elevator). “OnPass” - stop on arrival (otherwise there will be problems, and it will not stop)). Required parameters on the example of my configuration:
Let's move on to the “Func_button” setting, add the “env_texturetoggle” entity next to the button.

In "Target Brush(es)" we put the name "func_button" to which we want to attach the object (In order for the button to have animation when it is interacted with). Open the properties of the button. And add the following values to Outputs:
Flags (Toggles the exclusive functions of the object, its specific number is determined by the combination of added flags) buttons should look like this:
Don’t move (So that after pressing the button does not fly to another place, and remains static). Use Activates - So that the player can call the elevator on the “use (E)” button.
Do this operation with all similar objects. Then you can proceed to the final stage.
Go to the “VisGroups” window, uncheck everything except “Entities” 🡪 Select all entities 🡪 move to properties 🡪 and change the rendercolor value from “0 0 0” to “255 255 255” (Otherwise, all entities will be black, in [[GoldSource]] it worked differently). After that, open the Error Checking window and fix the remaining problems.
Preservation
Open the "File" menu, and choose one of the two: "Save" or "Save As...":
After compilation, the game will start. Now you can use the map in the new engine. Also don't forget to build cubemaps and set the lighting to HDR.
After the done actions, we can assume that the porting is completed. The new bsp is now running in [[Source]] Engine 24.

To create an elevator in the map, change the Entity from “func_train” to the new entity “func_tracktrain.” This brush, available in all Source games, functions as a moving platform that the player can ride on. It follows the path_track entities path, created by adding two “path_track” entities to the middle of the elevator and combining them
